TextENGNew: Oh right, I still had that new manga volume I bought.
I scanned my room, looking for where I'd tossed it.
If Mom were home, she'd nag me with, 'Reading manga again? Go study!'
Manga carries on the tradition of Utamaro and Hiroshige -- it's a genuine Japanese art form. ...Not that she'd listen even if I explained that.
Manga is Japanese culture!
...But to say that with any real conviction, Japanese manga would need to gain more recognition overseas.
changeReason: Old states confidence about overseas benefit; Japanese expresses a condition needed ('to say that proudly, manga would need more overseas recognition') -- opposite framing from assertion to requirement.

TextENGNew: I had a feeling that era wasn't too far off, though...
I was spacing out, mulling over these meaningless thoughts.
I'd only just gotten home a short while ago.
I'd started feeling sorry for Mion and her penalty game, so I'd carried half the bags for her.
The two of us walked through Hinamizawa side by side, hauling those massive shopping bags.
When we brought them to Rena's house, it turned out she was out running an errand.
I'd been planning to show Rena the enormous load Mion was lugging and tell her, 'Maybe go a little easier on her next time.' ...So it was kind of anticlimactic.
After that, I walked Mion back to the Sonozaki house, then headed home.
But... on the way to Rena's house... I'd steered the conversation toward what happened 'after the festival.'
...That was careless of me.
In other words, I'd gone and brought up the 'curse' -- the very topic Mion clearly didn't want to discuss.
I'll admit, ever since the night of the festival when Shion told me the details about the previous victims down by the riverbank, I hadn't been able to stop thinking about it.
But even so, it's hardly commendable to force the subject on someone who doesn't want to talk about it.
Still... those last words Mion had said.
She'd seemed deeply upset that I'd heard about the 'curse' from Shion, but...
What exactly... did she mean... by those words?
``...Shion's been getting too out of hand.@k@vS17/03/110300170. If she keeps snooping around like that... I suppose it can't be helped...``
What did she mean by 'out of hand'...?@k What was Shion supposedly snooping around about...?
From Mion's dimly visible silhouette, I thought I saw something indescribable rising up -- like a shimmering heat haze... But was that just my imagination?
The cries of the evening cicadas... strangely stirred up... my unease.
I happened to glance down and noticed a bookstore bag lying under my desk.
Ah, there it is -- the one I just bought. ...I muttered to myself as I reached for my favorite manga, but just then --
I heard the phone ringing downstairs.
And just when I was finally about to start reading...
changeReason: Old 'I debated if I should just keep reading' vs new 'just when I was about to start reading'; the JPN せっかく読もうと思ったのに means 'when I finally intended to read', so the new is more accurate.

TextENGNew: Reluctantly, I closed the book.
changeReason: Japanese 後ろ髪を引かれる思い is an idiom meaning 'reluctance'; old literally mistranslates as 'scratched the back of my head', missing the idiom entirely.

TextENGNew: ``--Hello, Kei-chan?@k@vS17/03/110300172. It's me, Mion.``
It was Mion, whom I'd just parted ways with.
``Oh, what were you up to just now?``
What was I doing, she asks... I looked down at what was in my hand --
changeReason: Old adds interpretation 'didn't necessarily have to tell her'; new follows the JPN more closely as the narrator trailing off while looking at what's in his hand.

TextENGNew: Huh...?
``Were you reading the new volume that just came out?``
changeReason: Old 'Have you read it yet?' (completed action) vs new 'Were you reading it?' (ongoing action) -- different question with different implications for the mind-reading scene.

TextENGNew: How did she know that...? I instinctively started looking around the room.
``And right about now, Kei-chan, you're frantically looking around, aren't you?@k@vS17/03/110300177. Aahahahahaha!``
...Is she watching me from somewhere? She was spot on, though...
changeReason: Old says 'That's impossible' (denial) while JPN 当たってるけどさ means 'She was spot on, though' (confirmation) — opposite meaning.

TextENGNew: I was equal parts annoyed and embarrassed.
``...Wh-what do you want?@k@vS17/01/110100132. Did you just call to mess with me?``
``Oh... are you mad?@k@vS17/03/110300179. Sorry, I was just playing around...``
Ah... maybe I was being a bit blunt there...
``...Hey, could we meet up? Like, right now?``
``What for? ...Didn't we just see each other?``
``Just now...?``
``Yeah, remember? The shopping was crazy, so I helped you carry half of it -- to Rena's place and then to yours.``
I hadn't expected the bags to be that heavy, though...
``...Oh, right.``
``So what's the deal? You miss me that much already~?``
I said it teasingly, as a joke.
But oddly enough, instead of firing back with one of her trademark retorts... there was only silence.
``Huh...? Mion?@k@vS17/01/110100137. What's wrong?``
But... she seemed a bit irritated... Well, whatever.
``...So, what is it?@k@vS17/01/110100139. Did you have something you wanted to talk about?``
``Sorry.@k@vS17/03/110300186. It's not something I can say over the phone...``
Something so important... she couldn't discuss it over the phone?
Right now, between the two of us, there was only one topic that would warrant that kind of secrecy.
I hesitated over whether to say it, but in the end, I spoke.
``Could it be... about the 'curse'...?``
There was a moment of silence.
``What if it were...?``
...Even if she said yes... what exactly was I expecting?
``...Don't you... want to hear about it?``
Saying I didn't want to hear it would be a lie.
But this was a subject Mion herself had been unwilling to discuss.
Why the sudden change of heart...? Did something happen to her?
``If you'd rather not, that's fine.@k@vS17/03/110300190. I won't force you...``
Wait! I never said... I didn't want to hear it.
``Then come to the library in Okinomiya.``
I had no choice but to agree. Even through the receiver, I could feel an invisible pressure bearing down on me.
I raced off on my bike.@k My mind was racing even faster.
``But why go all the way... to the Okinomiya library?``
Did that mean she couldn't talk about it anywhere around here? Was she really planning to reveal something that serious?
I told myself it couldn't be that extreme, but there was no denying my growing suspicion.
When I arrived at the library, Mion was already waiting at the entrance.
``Kei-chan.``
I raised my hand in response to her call, then couldn't help looking her up and down.
``What?@k@vS17/03/110300194. Is there something on me?``
``N-no, nothing.``
She was wearing her usual clothes. Nothing about Mion seemed particularly different.
``Mion.@k@vS17/01/110100144. Are you done with dinner preparations already?``
Mion looked puzzled for a moment.
``Why do you ask?``
Well, with all that shopping she'd done earlier, I figured she'd be making quite the dinner tonight...
``Oh, I finished that up before coming here.``
She said it so casually, like it was nothing. ...Could dinner really be made that quickly?
``My mom's a good cook too, but...``
Using that enormous quantity of ingredients would normally require a huge amount of time and effort.
``It's not that big a deal.@k@vS17/03/110300198. There were a lot of ingredients, sure, but it's just two of us living there.@k@vS17/03/110300199. Me and Gran.``
Come to think of it, she did say cooking was her specialty earlier.
``...So, what did you want to talk about?``
I looked Mion squarely in the face.
``Yeah.@k@vS17/03/110300201. ...Just as you guessed, Kei-chan... it's about 'the curse of Oyashiro-sama.'``
A cold current seemed to flow through the air... and an icy light flickered in Mion's eyes.
That fierce, bestial crimson gleam she'd shown me on the walk home earlier had receded into hiding.
I stared hard at Mion's face.
She'd been so reluctant before -- why had she suddenly decided to talk about this...?
Perhaps irritated by my lack of response, Mion furrowed her brow in displeasure.
``If you don't want to hear it, let's just drop it.``
My indecisive attitude seemed to disgust her further. Mion's expression grew increasingly fed up.
``No, it's just...@k@vS17/01/110100148. You seemed so opposed to talking about this before, so I was wondering what changed...@k@vS17/01/110100149. Did something happen...?``
``Not really.@k@vS17/03/110300204. If you don't want to hear it, forget it.``
Her feelings clearly hurt, Mion turned as though she was about to leave.
changeReason: Old 'expression grew more grim' vs new 'turned as though she was about to leave'; JPN 行ってしまおうとする means 'about to go/leave', which is an action, not just an expression change.

TextENGNew: Of course I wanted to hear it.@k I still didn't know what had prompted this, but the fact that Mion was finally willing to tell me about the curse -- that genuinely made me glad.
And more than anything... there's a saying about the fascination of the fearful... and my curiosity was winning out overwhelmingly.
``Wait, Mion.``
``Well?@k@vS17/03/110300206. Do you want to hear it or not?``
Her eyes were cold, looking down on me.@k The moment I met those eyes, a flash of defiance surged through me.
changeReason: Old 'I began to sulk' vs new 'a flash of defiance surged through me'; 'sulk' implies passive pouting while 'defiance' implies active resistance -- different emotional response.

TextENGNew: After coming all this way... there was no way I was going home empty-handed.
``Of course I want to hear it.@k@vS17/01/110100152. That's why I came all the way out here.``
``Then let's talk inside.``
Mion took the lead and walked into the library.
The reading area was deserted.@k We sat down in a corner.
Mion scanned the area once more to confirm no one else was around, then turned to face me.
``I'm only going to say this once.@k@vS17/03/110300209. So listen carefully.``
Her eyes gave off a chillingly cold light.
changeReason: Old 'A chill ran through me' describes narrator's sensation; new 'Her eyes gave off a chillingly cold light' describes Mion's appearance -- different subject entirely (JPN ゾッとするほど冷たい光だ describes the cold light).

TextENGNew: If I stared into them too long, it felt like my very blood might freeze...
changeReason: Subject swapped: old 'My blood froze as she stared into me' (she is actor) vs new 'If I stared into them too long' (I am actor); the JPN じっと見つめていると means 'if staring intently', making new more accurate.

TextENGNew: ``So I can't even ask you to repeat anything?``
changeReason: Japanese 聞き返す means 'to ask again/repeat'; old 'follow-up questions' implies new questions, while new 'ask you to repeat' is closer to the Japanese meaning.

TextENGNew: ``I'd rather you didn't.``
``And don't go digging into it afterward, either.``
``Hold on, Mion.@k@vS17/01/110100156. What do you mean, don't dig into it?``
``I mean don't research what you hear here... and absolutely don't go confirming it with anyone else.``
I swallowed the urge to argue back.
It would have been easy to push back.@k But the way she was pressing the point so emphatically -- there had to be a reason for it.
changeReason: Japanese 言い返す means 'to talk back/argue'; old 'responding to questions seemed simple' misinterprets this, while new 'It would have been easy to push back' is correct.

TextENGNew: Something must have happened in the past hour or so that made her feel the need to tell me this.
changeReason: Old predicts 'questions will arise during the hour'; new says 'something must have happened in the past hour' -- completely different temporal direction and meaning, with new matching the JPN 何か起きた (something happened).

TextENGNew: At any rate... if she was telling me not to pry, that meant there were records or documents somewhere that could be researched...
And that there were people in the village who still remembered these things.
changeReason: Old says 'I need to learn about this to become part of this village' (narrator's desire to belong) while JPN says 記憶している人が村にはいる (there are people in the village who still remember) — completely different content.

TextENGNew: Once that thought crossed my mind... being the kind of creature a human is... my curiosity was stoked all the more.
What was being hidden?@k What on earth were they trying to conceal?

``All right, I promise.@k@vS17/01/110100158. Whatever I hear here... I absolutely won't dig any further.@k@vS17/01/110100159. ...I won't try asking anyone about it.@k@vS17/01/110100160. ...Is that good enough?``
Mion nodded.
...Come to think of it, this was the second promise I'd made to Mion today.
The promise to stay away from Shion.
And the promise not to investigate what I was about to hear.
...Were these two promises contradictory? Or were they actually consistent?

``So this is about Oyashiro-sama's 'curse,' right?@k@vS17/01/110100162. In other words, something happened after Watanagashi...@k@vS17/01/110100163. Something happened again this year... and someone was sacrificed...?``
Mion stared into my eyes... and then slowly began to speak.
``You see... Tomitake-san, on the night of the Watanagashi festival...``
``...apparently, he died.``
``No, he's definitely dead.@k@vS17/03/110300217. I heard it from Gran...``
My mind went completely blank.
Wh... what...? Tomitake-san... what did she say?
I'd promised not to ask follow-up questions, so I kept repeating the question to myself, over and over.
Tomitake-san... what? Tomitake-san... what?! Tomitake-san... WHAT??!!
That question pounded relentlessly inside my skull, echoing and rebounding off the walls of my mind, hammering at me again and again!
Tomitake-san... WHAT??!?!?!!
Mion suddenly grabbed my shoulder.
changeReason: Japanese がっと掴んだ means 'grabbed forcefully'; old 'put a hand on' is gentle, while new 'grabbed' correctly conveys the forceful action.

TextENGNew: The abruptness of it locked my entire body rigid, but Mion leaned in close and spoke.
``Kei-chan, you understand... what this means, don't you?``
What does she mean by 'what this means'... I don't...
Not the cause of death, not why he died... not those kinds of reasons...
...What Mion meant was that he died on the day of Watanagashi...
In other words... this year, too... Oyashiro-sama's curse...!
It happened again!!!
``That's right. It happened...``
``Tomitake-san is dead.``
No... this is insane! This doesn't make any sense!
How can she say something like that so coldly, so matter-of-factly!!
What about our happy farewell?! Doesn't she remember?!!
How can she stay so calm while telling me this?!!
...Even so --
What did this mean? ...What the hell happened?
As those frantic questions whirled through my reeling mind, Mion began to tell me the details of the 'incident' involving Tomitake-san.
The first person to find him was a police officer who had finished security duty at the festival and was heading home.
It was 11:55 PM... just five minutes before midnight.
The location was... around where the road toward town transitions into pavement... at the bottom of the hill... on the side of the road.
``...In the darkness, there was nothing but moonlight and the car's headlights...``
I knew that road. There were almost no streetlights.
``When the headlights swept across the road, a blood-soaked corpse suddenly appeared in the light.``
If that car hadn't happened to pass by, the body probably wouldn't have been found until the next morning...
Tomitake-san's discovery must have been pure coincidence.
Just imagining it made my skin crawl.
...Tomitake-san lying face-down on the road, drenched in blood.@k ...Blood and filth spread all across the asphalt...
According to the officer, his first thought was that it was a hit-and-run.
But... when he went to check whether the man was still alive, he immediately noticed something abnormal.
``...His throat... had been completely torn to shreds.``
W-with a knife... or something?!
The words almost left my mouth, but a wave of revulsion forced them back down.
Even so, my meaning must have gotten across. Mion, who had been looking down, raised her piercing eyes to meet mine.
``With his own fingernails.``
Finger... nails...@k What??!!
The nails on his fingers?!
``Scrape... scrape... scrape... scrape... Like that.``
...Scraping??!!
A horrible sound echoed deep inside my ears.
*scrape* *scrape* *scrape* *scrape* *scrape* *scrape* *scrape* *scrape* *scrape*
So... Tomitake-san wasn't murdered...
``Suicide??!?!!``
That was the police's conclusion as well.
I sat there in stunned silence, thinking.
After parting ways with us, Tomitake-san suddenly started clawing at his own throat! Raking at it wildly with his own fingernails.
...Tearing through the skin, blood spraying out... and still he wouldn't stop. Scraping... scraping!!
``His nails had been almost entirely peeled off, apparently.``
Peeled off!!!
With that kind of tremendous force... scraping! Scraping!!
And then... even tearing into the veins and arteries... Scrape! *Pop!* Scrape!
...Spraying fresh blood in every direction!
Vomiting blood. Retching. ...And then collapsing... convulsing... and dying.
Despite such a grotesquely abnormal manner of death, no traces of any drugs were found in his system.
``It wasn't drug-related...?``
Mion calmly and clearly shook her head no.
But... was this really suicide?!
I'd never heard of anything like this!!
...This isn't normal... This is not a normal way to die...!!!
If this isn't a bizarre death, what is...?
Of all the deaths in the past five years... this was the one that most resembled an actual curse...!
The scene was even more disturbing.@k Apparently, a piece of squared lumber was found lying by the body's feet.
Tomitake-san's blood was smeared all over it... and the wounds on his hands matched the shape of the wood.
...There were impact marks on the surrounding trees and guardrail. Tomitake-san's blood was scattered all around...
...So what did that mean???

``What it means is... even as Tomitake-san was clawing at his throat with his nails...@k@vS17/03/110300228. dripping blood, drenched in it from head to toe...@k@vS17/03/110300229. he was swinging a piece of lumber around with his other hand...``
Was he trying to defend himself against someone??
To my unspoken question, Mion silently nodded.
On top of that, numerous wounds were found all over his body -- injuries clearly inflicted by others.
``...It seems Tomitake-san was assaulted. By multiple people... ganging up on him.``
Tomitake-san was surrounded and attacked.
He fled in terror through the dark road, but when he could run no more, he grabbed a discarded piece of lumber to fight back...
...All the while, in a frenzy, clawing at his own throat. Scraping and scraping!!
And then... he died.
The silence felt like it would stretch on forever.
Then Mion suddenly broke the suffocating silence.
With words I never expected --
``Because of all this... we're under suspicion again.``
She said it quietly.
...Under suspicion? ...Of what? ...By whom? ...For what? ...Why?
After hearing the horrific account of Tomitake-san's death, this sudden non sequitur left me doubting my ears.

``The police believe... that the Sonozaki family has been pulling the strings behind every incident.@k@vS17/03/110300233. ......Because all the victims of Watanagashi night were people who were inconvenient for the Sonozaki family.``
People connected to the Sonozaki family... the pro-dam faction.
Throughout Hinamizawa's history, the people who'd sided with the dam supporters during the dam war had been dying and disappearing, one after another.
``Every year on Watanagashi, an enemy is struck down... That alone is enough to draw suspicion.``
When you think about it...
First it was the dam construction foreman... Then the villagers who'd supported the dam...
People who'd tried to build the dam had been meeting bizarre deaths, one after another.
The causes of death varied... but every single victim had been a dam supporter...
In other words, they had all been enemies of the Sonozaki family...
There was no hard evidence... only rumors and circumstantial suspicion.
But... it was awfully hard to simply laugh it off.
But... hold on... what about the shrine priest who died, and the housewife who was killed the year after?!
``Their connection to the Sonozaki family was tenuous at best...?``
``Right... tenuous... and yet...``
Wh... what??
Mion's eyes suddenly took on a sorrowful quality, yet at the same time harbored a cold light, as if she were placing a curse.
``That housewife... was beaten to death.``
I already knew that. Shion had told me about the incident.
``And if I remember right... a child from that family... disappeared afterward, right?``
``...That child was actually in the same school as us until last year.``
In our same school... until last year...?!
``His name was Satoshi Houjou-kun...@k@vS17/03/110300239. He was in our class.@k@vS17/03/110300240. ......He was Satoko's older brother.``
It felt like the air around me had suddenly grown thin.
My mouth moved wordlessly.
Like a goldfish in a bowl where the oxygen was running out, desperately gasping for air.
Satoko's grief-stricken eyes flashed before me, then faded away.
This couldn't be. Satoko had... lost her brother to the curse...?
Even people this close to me were being swept up in these incidents under the name of the 'curse'?!
A sudden tremor of terror seized me, and my mind began to race.
Wait a moment.
If the connection was supposedly tenuous, then what about Tomitake-san?! He wasn't even remotely connected to the dam conflict!!
Isn't it absurd for someone to be killed just for being an outsider??!

``Exactly.@k@vS17/03/110300242. At first, the targets were clearly connected to the dam construction project.@k@vS17/03/110300243. But as time went on, the victims' 'degree of connection' has been growing thinner and thinner...``
``So, in other words...``
It doesn't matter whether you're connected to the dam conflict or not -- as long as you're inconvenient for the Sonozaki family...?!
Satoshi must have been inconvenient for the Sonozaki family too.
Maybe he learned one of the Sonozaki family's secrets, for example... And so he was eliminated...
An icy chill raced down my spine.@k ...I couldn't speak for a long time.
I could barely even swallow the saliva pooling in the back of my throat.
Even when I managed it, the gulp echoed loudly in my ears...
False accusations... groundless suspicion... fabricated evidence born of absurdity.
But wait. Were they really false accusations...? Were they truly groundless?
I stared hard at Mion's face. She instantly averted her gaze.
If Satoshi was eliminated because he'd learned something he shouldn't have...? If Tomitake-san's death was connected to the same thing...? ......Then what about me?
Hadn't I, just now, learned something inconvenient for the Sonozaki family?
``...Why are you telling me something like this?``
``I wanted you to understand why this isn't something I can easily talk about.@k@vS17/03/110300245. ...But you absolutely must keep this secret from the other club members.``
Why did it have to be kept secret...?
``Everyone is extremely sensitive about the 'curse.'@k@vS17/03/110300247. They're all trying to make sure the harm doesn't come their way.``
I gave a vague nod. I could certainly tell from the atmosphere that everyone wanted to avoid the topic... But still...
``Also... especially in front of Satoko, I don't want to talk about any of this.``
Of course. No one would want to hear about a 'curse' that had taken their own brother from them... But...

``Understood?@k@vS17/03/110300250. So everything about us meeting here and talking -- it stays absolutely secret from everyone.@k@vS17/03/110300251. ...If you break that promise... you'll regret it, Kei-chan.``
Mion's clear eyes gleamed with a blade-sharp light, cold as a sword of ice.
Chillingly cold.
I had no way to counter those eyes.
``G-got it.@k@vS17/01/110100175. Everything that happened here stays between us.@k@vS17/01/110100176. I swear it...``
Just then, the closing announcement echoed through the library.
Mion and I left the library.
``...This old man has to go visit some relatives now.``
With that, Mion gave me a wave and we parted ways in front of the library. I started walking in the opposite direction.
Just then, a familiar voice called out to me.
``Keiichi-ku~n!``
I froze and spun around.
It was Rena, straddling her bicycle. There were shopping bags in her bike basket.
``...What are you doing all the way out here?``
``Oh, well...@k@vS17/01/110100178. I just thought it'd be nice to visit the library for a change.``
``Oh really...``
``What's with that look?``
``...It is a nice, cool place to hang out, isn't it?``
``Plus, it's free.``
``Ahahahaha, that's true~!``
``...Well, see you tomorrow.``
I wanted to get out of there as quickly as possible.
I headed for the bike rack, but for some reason Rena moved to block my path.
``Why are you in such a rush? ...A rush?``
``I-I'm not in a rush or anything.``
``Hmm... really? ...Really?``
``It's just getting dark, so I figured I should head home.``
``...Then let's ride home together.``
She tilted her head slightly and looked up at me from below.
Those eyes were clearly suspicious.
``Wh-what?``
She kept staring at me in silence. It made me uncomfortable.
``I was just wondering what you were up to at the library.``
``Studying. Just studying.``
As I said that, I casually glanced toward the library. Mion was already gone.
What Mion had told me earlier was still gnawing at me.
What was she thinking, telling me such a grave secret...?
Mion was worried about being suspected by the police, and that had made her anxious. That much I could understand. I'd do whatever I could to support her. But...
If knowing things you shouldn't know was a crime... if learning something inconvenient for the Sonozaki family was a crime, and you'd be 'eliminated' in the name of the curse...
Then wouldn't I... be the next one eliminated?!
``Hey, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS17/02/110200065. Weren't you here with someone? ...With someone?``
Wh-what?!!
``...I thought I saw you with someone a little while ago.``
``I-I was alone.``
``Really?``
``Really.``
``But what?``
``I thought I saw a girl with long hair standing next to you.``
She saw...?!
I absolutely had to cover this up... That's what I was thinking.
``Oh... r-right, that was... just the person sitting next to me at the reading table...``
``Hmm... is that so?``
Rena studied my face intently.
``What is it?``
``It's just, even if she was sitting next to you, why would you both walk out together...?``
``It... it just happened to be the same time we both left.``
``You're lying.``
``Keiichi-kun, you've definitely known that girl for a while.``
``Wh-what makes you say that?!``
``Because the way you acted around her was completely natural, Keiichi-kun.``
``I-I'm always natural.``
``Sure. But then that doesn't add up, does it?``
``Wh... what doesn't add up?``
``If she were really a stranger, why would you be so relaxed around her?``
I flinched.
``Because, Keiichi-kun, you're the self-conscious type.@k@vS17/02/110200078. You tend to get stiff and awkward around people you don't know.``
...She was right. I do have trouble talking naturally with strangers at first...
``And also...``
Also?
``Keiichi-kun, your nose twitches when you're lying.``
Wha...?! I clapped my hand over my nose.
Did I really have a habit like that...??
...And then Rena suddenly started snickering.
``Got you!``
Sh-she got me...
``So it wasn't really a stranger, was it?``
I was completely speechless.
Left with no choice, I...
``Actually, she's my age... we just hit it off talking...@k@vS17/01/110100198. We became friends recently.``
...I piled on an even bigger lie.
``Is she from Okinomiya?``
Rena nodded with a 'hmm,' but kept pressing.
``Y-yeah, I think so.``
``What school does she go to?``
Huh? ...I don't know any of the middle schools in Okinomiya.
While agonizing over having to keep spouting nonsense, I desperately tried to think of something.
``N-no... I never asked her...``
``You never even asked what school she goes to?@k@vS17/02/110200086. That's kind of odd... hm...?``
Rena tilted her head to the side in an exaggerated manner, and then...
...leaned in with a teasing grin, peering at my face.
``You're lying. I knew it.``
I finally realized that I'd fallen completely into Rena's trap.
Even so, I couldn't admit that I'd been with Mion.

``Got it?@k@vS17/03/110300250. So, everything we've discussed here absolutely must remain a secret from everyone.@k@vS17/03/110300251. ...If you break that promise... I'm sure you'll regret it, Kei-chan.``
My promise to Mion continued echoing in the back of my mind.
My expression must have been contorted, as though I'd bitten into something bitter.
I was desperately trying to figure out how to wriggle out of this.
...Then, suddenly, Rena burst out laughing.
``Ahahahaha, Keiichi-kun, I'm just joking~!``
Smack! She gave me a hearty slap on the shoulder.
...Wh-what??!!
``Could it be... love at first sight?``
``Okay, I get it.@k@vS17/02/110200091. I won't pry any further.``
So... she gets what, exactly???!!
``But wow, I'm surprised, Keiichi-kun... Ahahahahaha~!``
...Surprised?
What... is so surprising...???
``Don't worry.@k@vS17/02/110200094. Rena won't tell a soul!``
Rena hopped onto her bicycle and pedaled off, laughing all the way.
I scrambled over to my own bike.
We pedaled toward Hinamizawa together.
The entire way home, Rena kept laughing and teasing me nonstop.
At any rate... I seemed to have somehow managed to keep my secret.
But no matter how much Rena laughed and chattered, the only word reverberating through my head was 'curse.'
The whole ride home, pedaling my bike, I was consumed by regret.
I wished... I had never listened to what Mion told me --
